title: Honoring Both Traditions
content: Planned a quinceañera for a girl whose mom was Mexican and dad was Jewish. The challenge? Honor BOTH traditions. We had the last doll ceremony AND a hora. Mariachi AND a DJ playing Hava Nagila. Her grandmother cried. Both of them. That's what celebration should be. Bringing worlds together.
extended_content: She was worried it would feel like two separate parties. I told her: 'Your identity isn't one or the other. It's both. Let's celebrate both.' We wove the traditions together. The court presentation had elements of both cultures. The father-daughter dance blended into a hora. By the end, you couldn't tell where one tradition ended and the other began. That's the magic of good planning - it honors who people really are.
lesson: Celebrations should honor who you are
